recalls.Description:
The recall affects Tomum Minoxidil Hair Growth Treatment spray bottles, which are 100 mL in size. These bottles feature a silver design with a blue wraparound label and a black cap, and they are packaged in a blue box labeled with "TOMUM" and "Hair Growth Treatment" on the front. The product poses a serious risk as it does not comply with child-resistant packaging standards mandated by the Poison Prevention Packaging Act.

The non-child-resistant packaging can lead to severe injury or even death if ingested by young children. Consumers are urged to act swiftly to ensure safety and obtain a replacement for the recalled product.
recalls.Title: Tomum Minoxidil Hair Serum Spray Bottles Recalled Due to Child Safety Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For more information, contact Belleka at 862 244-1785 from 9 a.m. to 4 p.m. ET Monday through Friday, email tomumcs@gmail.com, or visit https://itomum.com/contact-us/ or https://itomum.com.

Products.Name: Tomum Minoxidil Hair Growth Treatment Spray Bottles

Images.Caption: Recalled Tomum Minoxidil Hair Regrowth Treatment (Front view)
Recalled Tomum Minoxidil -Hair Regrowth Treatment (Rear view)

Retailers.Name: Available online at Amazon.com from March 2025 to September 2025 for approximately $20.

Distributors.Name: Belleka Inc, dba TOMUM, of China
Chifengrongxiangshangmaoyouxiangongsi, dba Haircare Solution, of China
SOLUVIA LLC, of Brooklyn, New York, US
Xingchang Technology Co., Ltd., dba Xingchang, of China

Hazards.Name: Non-child-resistant packaging poses a poisoning risk to children.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should keep the recalled bottles away from children and contact Belleka for a free replacement, including two child-resistant bottles (60 mL each). They will need to dispose of the recalled bottle and send a photo of it in the trash to Belleka at tomumcs@gmail.com.

recalls.metaDescription: Recall of Tomum Minoxidil Hair Serum due to child safety risks. Contact Belleka for a replacement and keep bottles away from children.

recalls.Description: Joly's is recalling its 80% Vinegar sold in 32-ounce packs of two due to significant safety hazards. The product is packaged in a transparent bottle with a white cap and features an orange and red label displaying the brand name, product type, and dilution information. However, it lacks essential warning labels and first-aid instructions, violating federal safety regulations.
recalls.Title: Joly's 80% Vinegar Recall Due to Serious Poisoning and Chemical Burn Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Joly's at 240-412-8720 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or via email at jolysllc@gmail.com.

Products.Name: Joly's 80% Vinegar (32 oz, pack of two)

Retailers.Name: Available online at Amazon.com from May 2025 to November 2025 for approximately $23.

Distributors.Name: Joly's LLC, dba Josie'Store, of Orlando, Florida

Hazards.Name: Lack of required warning labels and first-aid instructions, posing risks of poisoning and chemical burns.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should immediately store the vinegar out of children's reach and contact Joly's for a full refund and disposal guidance.

recalls.metaDescription: Joly's recalls 80% Vinegar due to serious poisoning and burn risks. Contact Joly's for refunds and disposal instructions.

recalls.Description:
Scepter B62 gas and oil fuel containers are being recalled due to serious safety concerns. These containers, which feature a red body, black caps, and yellow spouts, have a dual-chamber design with a 6-liter capacity for gasoline and a 2.5-liter capacity for oil. The containers are marked with "Scepter" and "Essence Danger" on the side. The lack of required safety features poses a risk of flash fires and potential poisoning hazards for children. 
Consumers are urged to stop using these containers immediately and keep them out of children's reach. Mameix Retail is offering a full refund, and customers will need to mark the containers as "Recalled" and provide a photo of the discarded item to complete the return process.
recalls.Title: Scepter Gas and Oil Fuel Containers Recalled Over Fire and Child Safety Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Mameix Retail at 587-816-2804 from 11 a.m. to 5 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday, or email scepterrecall@gmail.com.

Products.Name: Scepter Gas and Oil Fuel Containers

Images.Caption: Recalled Scepter B62 Gas and Oil Fuel Container – front
Recalled Scepter B62 Gas and Oil Fuel Container – front

Retailers.Name: Available for purchase on Amazon.com from August 2024 through May 2025 for approximately $40.

Hazards.Name: Risk of flash fire due to missing flame mitigation devices and potential child poisoning from non-child-resistant spouts.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the containers, keep them away from children, and contact Mameix Retail for a refund.

recalls.metaDescription: Scepter gas and oil fuel containers recalled due to fire hazards and child safety risks. Contact Mameix Retail for refunds and safety instructions.

recalls.Description: GM Gumili Wintergreen Essential Oil is being recalled due to a serious risk of poisoning for children. The product is packaged in an amber glass bottle with a black screw-on cap and features a white label displaying the "GM" logo along with the product name and an image of a wintergreen plant. The essential oil is sold in a white box with blue text that includes safety instructions and the manufacturer's details on the back. A black dropper cap is included with the bottle.
recalls.Title: GM Gumili Wintergreen Essential Oil Recall Due to Child Poisoning Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For more information, consumers can reach Linkers at 347-775-2310 from 9 a.m. to 6 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday, email admin@linkersecomm.com, or visit us.shoprythm.com and click on "Recall" at the top of the page.

Images.Caption: Recalled GM Gumili Wintergreen Essential Oil-Bottle and Box Front
Recalled GM Gumili Wintergreen Essential Oil-Bottle Side Label
Recalled GM Gumili Wintergreen Essential Oil-Bottle Label with UPC Code

Retailers.Name: Available online at Amazon.com by Gumili Beauty from October 2023 through November 2025 for approximately $8.

Distributors.Name: Guangzhou Feifan Kuajing Supply Chain Co., Ltd. dba Gumili Beauty, of China

Hazards.Name: The essential oil contains methyl salicylate, which must be packaged in child-resistant containers as mandated by the Poison Prevention Packaging Act (PPPA). The current packaging does not meet this requirement, creating a poisoning hazard for young children.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should immediately keep the recalled essential oil out of children's reach and contact the company for a full refund or a complimentary replacement bottle. To process the refund or replacement, consumers will need to send a photo of the product disposed of in the trash to gumilius@outlook.com.

recalls.metaDescription: GM Gumili Wintergreen Essential Oil is recalled due to child poisoning risks. Learn how to secure a refund or replacement.

recalls.Description: Organic Zing Birch Essential Oil bottles are being recalled due to a serious risk of poisoning in children. The amber bottles feature black caps and have a beige and green label displaying the Organic Zing logo, along with the words "Birch Oil" and "Pure & Natural." The packaging also indicates that the product is made in India. This recall is prompted by the failure to meet mandatory child-resistant packaging standards, as required by the Poison Prevention Packaging Act (PPPA).
recalls.Title: Recall of Organic Zing Birch Essential Oil Due to Child Poisoning Hazard
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers should immediately secure the recalled bottles out of children's reach and contact Linkers at 347-775-2310 from 9 a.m. to 6 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday. They can also email admin@linkersecomm.com or visit us.shoprythm.com and click on the "Recall" link for further details.

Distributors.Name: Linkers, of India

Hazards.Name: Risk of poisoning due to non-child-resistant packaging containing methyl salicylate.

Remedies.Name: Return the essential oil for a full refund and follow instructions for safe disposal.

recalls.metaDescription: Recall of Organic Zing Birch Essential Oil due to child poisoning risk. Secure bottles and contact Linkers for a refund.

recalls.Description: Zan International Trading has issued a recall for Lotus Joy Organic Sweet Birch Essential Oil due to safety concerns regarding child poisoning. The product is packaged in 0.34 fluid ounce amber bottles featuring a black cap and a separate glass dropper. The label displays the brand name 'LotusJoy' along with details about the essential oil, including its ingredients and usage instructions. The outer packaging is a blue box with similar branding and information.
recalls.Title: Zan International Trading Recalls Lotus Joy Sweet Birch Essential Oil Due to Child Poisoning Hazard
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Zan International Trading at 888-5739-288 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or via email at zantradellc@hotmail.com.

recalls.Description: Bazic Products has issued a recall for its Silicone Glue, Model 2030, due to serious safety concerns. The glue is packaged in a clear plastic bottle featuring a blue nozzle and a blue and black label that includes instructions and warnings. The label prominently displays "Bazic", "Silicone Glue", and "Clear Adhesive Sealant", with the batch code "V01233-031725-70004523" located near the barcode on the side. Only this specific batch is affected by the recall.
recalls.Title: Bazic Products Recalls Silicone Glue Due to Poisoning Risk; Fails Child Safety Standards
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Bazic Products at 877-772-2942 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. PT Monday through Friday or via email at compliance@bazic.com.

Images.Caption: Recalled Bazic Silicone Glue – Front and Side
Recalled Bazic Silicone Glue – Location of Batch Code

Retailers.Name: Available at Shims Bargain Inc., Henry Lamond Co. Ltd., Universal Wholesale, and D'notions & crafts stores across the nation, as well as online at Amazon.com and Walmart.com from May 2025 through October 2025 for approximately $3.

Importers.Name: Bangkit USA Inc, dba Bazic Products, of El Monte, California

Hazards.Name: The glue contains methanol and is not packaged in child-resistant containers as mandated by the Poison Prevention Packaging Act. This poses a significant risk of serious injury or death from poisoning if ingested by children. Furthermore, the product does not meet labeling requirements under the Federal Hazardous Substances Act, as it is incorrectly labeled "Non-Toxic" and lacks a warning for its flammability.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should immediately stop using the recalled Bazic Silicone Glue and contact Bazic Products for guidance on submitting a photo of the product to obtain a full refund. It is important to dispose of the recalled glue according to local and state regulations. Consumers should not dispose of methanol in sewers, rainwater pipes, or regular trash to prevent fire hazards and environmental damage.

recalls.metaDescription: Bazic Products recalls Silicone Glue due to poisoning risk. Learn about the safety concerns and how to get a refund.

recalls.Description: Viofairy Hair Growth Serum is being recalled due to a serious safety risk. The product, packaged in a black bottle with a gold and white label, contains 5% minoxidil with biotin. Unfortunately, the packaging does not meet child-resistant standards, which poses a significant risk of poisoning if ingested by young children. This violation of the Poison Prevention Packaging Act necessitates immediate action.
recalls.Title: Viofairy Hair Growth Serum Recalled Due to Child Poisoning Hazard
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers should secure the recalled serum out of children's reach and contact FDAF at sesuall@outlook.com for instructions on disposing of the product and obtaining a free replacement.

recalls.Description: Mindbodygreen is recalling its Ultimate Multivitamin+ dietary supplement bottles due to a serious safety concern. These eight-ounce amber glass bottles, containing 60 capsules, do not comply with the Poison Prevention Packaging Act, as they lack child-resistant caps. This poses a significant risk of poisoning if young children access the supplements. The affected products have specific UPCs and lot numbers printed on the bottles, which were manufactured between September 2021 and March 2025.
recalls.Title: Mindbodygreen Recalls Ultimate Multivitamin+ Due to Child Poisoning Risk from Non-Child-Resistant Packaging
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers can reach out to Mindbodygreen via email at multi@mindbodygreen.com, or visit shop.mindbodygreen.com/pages/multi-update for further details. Additional information is also available at www.shop.mindbodygreen.com under "Important Multi packaging update".

Hazards.Name: Non-child-resistant packaging leading to potential poisoning risk for children.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should secure the bottles out of children's reach and request a free child-resistant cap from Mindbodygreen.

recalls.metaDescription: Mindbodygreen recalls Ultimate Multivitamin+ due to child poisoning risks from non-child-resistant packaging. Secure your supplements now.

This hair serum is packaged in a black glass bottle featuring a black and golden dropper cap and a label that reads "SEFUDUN HAIR GROWTH SERUM" and "BIOTIN 5% MINOXIDIL." Each kit also includes a black plastic applicator. The lack of child-resistant packaging poses a significant risk of poisoning for young children if ingested.
recalls.Title: Sefudun 5% Minoxidil Hair Growth Serum Kits Recalled for Child Safety Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Nengmiaokeji at sesuall@outlook.com.

recalls.Description: The recall affects Iristar Minoxidil Spray Bottles, which contain 5% minoxidil and biotin intended for hair and beard growth. These products are packaged in black plastic bottles featuring navy blue labels and white accents, with a spray cap. Both the bottles and their packaging prominently display the brand name 'Iristar' and 'Minoxidil Spray', while usage instructions and ingredient details are located on the reverse side.

recalls.Description:
RootStim is recalling its Beard Growth Serum spray bottles due to a serious safety hazard. The product, packaged in black boxes featuring orange and white text and an orange mustache icon, is labeled as 'Beard Growth Treatment' with '5% Minoxidil - Biotin & Rice Water'. The black bottles have a black spray cap and an orange and black wraparound label, which contains the same information as the box. The packaging does not meet mandatory child-resistant standards, posing a risk of poisoning to young children if ingested.

Consumers are urged to keep these serum bottles out of children's reach and contact RootStim for guidance on obtaining a replacement. The original bottles must be discarded as part of the remedy process. This recall highlights the importance of child safety in product packaging, especially for items containing potentially harmful substances.
recalls.Title: RootStim Beard Growth Serum Spray Bottles Recalled Due to Child Poisoning Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For more information, contact RootStim at 202-743-1714 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, email finergraceing@gmail.com, or visit https://rootstim.com and click 'Contact Us'.

Hazards.Name: Risk of serious injury or death from poisoning due to non-child-resistant packaging

Remedies.Name: Secure recalled bottles and contact RootStim for replacement instructions

recalls.Description:
Ruahouine is recalling its Hair Growth Serum due to a serious safety risk for children. The product is sold in 60ml (2.11 fl. oz.) transparent amber dropper bottles, labeled with "Ruahouine," "Minoxidil Hair Growth Serum," and "5% Minoxidil With Biotin." The packaging does not comply with the required child-resistant standards, posing a danger of poisoning if ingested by young children.

Consumers are urged to take immediate action by securing the recalled serum bottles and keeping them out of children's reach. Ruahouine is providing instructions for the safe disposal of the product and offering refunds or replacements for affected customers.
recalls.Title: Recall of Ruahouine Hair Growth Serum Due to Child Safety Hazard
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Ruahouine via email at ruahouine@gmail.com.

The product, packaged in a one-ounce dark amber bottle with a black dropper cap, is labeled as "Beard Growth Oil," "Feel The Beard," and contains "5% minoxidil with biotin." The packaging does not meet child-resistant standards, which poses a serious risk of poisoning if ingested by children. This recall is in compliance with the Poison Prevention Packaging Act.

recalls.Description:
Plantimex is recalling Mamisan Pain Relieving Topical Ointment due to safety concerns regarding its packaging. The ointment is sold in a 3.52-ounce orange plastic jar with a white lid, featuring the Mamisan trademark. This recall specifically affects jars with the UPC code 860006498115. The packaging does not meet the required child-resistant standards, increasing the risk of serious injury or death from accidental ingestion by children.

Consumers are urged to keep the recalled ointment out of children's reach and contact Plantimex for a free replacement lid. After securing the ointment with the new lid, it can be used as intended. This recall highlights the importance of proper packaging to prevent potential poisoning incidents.
recalls.Title: Plantimex Issues Recall for Mamisan Lidocaine Ointment Due to Child Poisoning Hazard
recalls.ConsumerContact: For more information, contact Plantimex at 855-752-6869 from 9 a.m. to 4 p.m. PT, Monday through Friday, or email customercare@plantimexusa.com with the subject "RECALL". Visit http://plantimexusa.com/contact.php or www.plantimexusa.com for further assistance.

Recalled Mamisan Lidocaine Ointment 3.52 ounce jar

Distributors.Name: Plantimex Distributors, Inc., of San Diego, California

Hazards.Name: Non-child-resistant packaging posing a poisoning risk to children

Remedies.Name: Free replacement lid for secure packaging

recalls.metaDescription: Plantimex recalls Mamisan Lidocaine Ointment due to child safety hazards. Find details on securing your product and obtaining a replacement lid.

recalls.Description: Demlar is recalling its MoonSoll and Magic Chems fuel bottles, which are used in various heating appliances like tabletop fire pits and stoves. These bottles, available in 500mL and 1000mL sizes, feature a clear design with either a red or green cap and display the brand name and capacity on the label. They were sold individually or in packs of three. The recall is prompted by serious safety concerns related to flash fire risks due to non-compliance with federal safety standards.
recalls.Title: Demlar Recalls MoonSoll and Magic Chems Fuel Bottles Due to Fire Hazard and Safety Violations
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Demlar at 943-212-3054 from 10 a.m. to 6 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or email info@demlar.com. More details can be found online at https://demlar.com/recall-1 or by visiting Demlar.com and clicking on the 'RECALL' link at the top of the page.

Hazards.Name: Risk of flash fire due to lack of flame mitigation devices and misleading safety claims.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the bottles immediately, keep them away from children, and contact Demlar for a full refund.

recalls.Description: F&F Fine Wines has announced a recall for specific glass bottles of Kirkland Signature Valdobbiadene Prosecco DOCG due to a potential laceration hazard. These bottles are identifiable by their green color, purple foil tops, and purple labels that read 'Kirkland Signature Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore DOCG.' The affected UPC is 196633883742 and the Costco Item Number is 1879870.
recalls.Title: F&F Fine Wines Issues Recall for Kirkland Signature Valdobbiadene Prosecco DOCG Bottles Sold at Costco Due to Laceration Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers are urged to contact Ethica Wines at 786-810-7132, available Monday through Friday from 8 a.m. to 6 p.m. EST, or via email at customercare@ethicawines.com.

Products.Name: Kirkland Signature Valdobbiadene Prosecco DOCG Bottles

Images.Caption: Recalled Kirkland Signature Valdobbiadene Prosecco DOCG bottle

Injuries.Name: F&F Fine Wines International, Inc. has received ten reports of the bottles shattering or breaking, resulting in one laceration injury.

Retailers.Name: Costco locations across IA, IL, IN, KY, MI, MN, MO, ND, NE, OH, SD, and WI from April 2025 to August 2025, priced around $8.

Importers.Name: F&F Fine Wines International, Inc. dba Ethica Wines, of Miami, Florida

Hazards.Name: Risk of laceration due to breaking or shattering bottles

Remedies.Name: Immediately cease use of the recalled bottles, do not attempt to open them, discard them in household trash, and reach out to Ethica Wines for refund instructions.

recalls.metaDescription: Recall alert: Kirkland Signature Valdobbiadene Prosecco DOCG bottles sold at Costco pose a laceration hazard. Act now for a refund.

recalls.Description: Wilteexs has issued a recall for its bioethanol fuel bottles, which are designed for use in tabletop fire pits, fireplaces, stoves, and burners. These clear bottles, holding 1.06 quarts (1 liter) of fuel, feature the branding "WILTEEXS" and "BIO ETHANOL" along with an image of a flame. The product code "X004IQ9ZCH" can be found below the barcode on the packaging. It is crucial to dispose of any recalled fuel according to local regulations and environmental guidelines to prevent fire hazards and pollution.
recalls.Title: Wilteexs Bioethanol Fuel Bottles Recalled Due to Fire Hazard and Safety Violations
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach out to Wilteexs via email at hongyaodemg@outlook.com.

Images.Caption: Recalled Wilteexs Bioethanol Fuel Bottle
"X004IQ9ZCH" is printed below the barcode on the back of the recalled Wilteexs bioethanol fuel bottles

Retailers.Name: Available on Amazon from April 2025 to July 2025 for approximately $17.

Importers.Name: Hongyaodekejiyouxiangongsi, dba online seller Wilteexs, of China

Hazards.Name: The ethanol fuel bottles do not comply with federal safety standards for portable fuel containers, lacking necessary flame mitigation devices, which increases the risk of flash fires. Additionally, they contain misleading claims of being "Non Toxic," violating federal regulations.

Remedies.Name: Consumers are advised to cease using the recalled bottles immediately, keep them away from children, and contact Wilteexs for a full refund. They should mark the bottles with "Do not use" and their Amazon order number, then send a photo to hongyaodemg@outlook.com. Proper disposal should follow local and state regulations.

recalls.metaDescription: Wilteexs recalls bioethanol fuel bottles due to fire hazards. Immediate action required for safety and refunds.

recalls.Description: Almpal has issued a recall for its dissolved oxygen test kits due to a serious safety concern. These kits, packaged in a cream-colored box with a white label, contain a bottle of sulfuric acid that is not child-resistant, violating safety standards. This poses a significant risk of poisoning if young children access the contents.
recalls.Title: Almpal Dissolved Oxygen Test Kits Recalled Due to Poisoning Risk for Children
recalls.ConsumerContact: For further details, consumers can reach out to Almpal via email at lehapet@hotmail.com.

Images.Caption: Recalled Almpal Dissolved Oxygen Test Kit - Closed
Recalled Almpal Dissolved Oxygen Test Kit - Opened

Retailers.Name: Available on Amazon from May 2025 to August 2025, priced around $25.

Distributors.Name: Ningbo Leha E-Commerce Co., Ltd., of China doing business as Amazon seller Almpal

Hazards.Name: Risk of serious injury or death from poisoning due to non-child-resistant sulfuric acid packaging.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should keep the kits out of children's reach, contact Almpal for disposal instructions, and request a full refund after submitting a photo of the product.

recalls.metaDescription: Almpal recalls dissolved oxygen test kits due to poisoning risks for children. Learn how to dispose of them safely and request a refund.

recalls.Description: Blueroot Health is recalling specific Bariatric Fusion dietary supplement bottles due to a serious risk of child poisoning. The recall affects high ADEK multivitamin capsules in 90 and 270-count bottles, as well as One Per Day bariatric multivitamin capsules in 90-count bottles, all containing 45mg of iron. The affected bottles are distinguished by their white and orange design and feature smooth caps without the required "push down & turn" embossed lettering. Lot numbers 0066J4, 0065J4, 0453B5, or 0370B5 can be found on the bottom of these bottles.
recalls.Title: Blueroot Health Issues Recall for Bariatric Fusion Iron Multivitamins Due to Child Poisoning Hazard
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers should contact Blueroot Health at 866-259-0602 from 8 a.m. to 6 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday. Alternatively, they can email support@bariatricfusion.com or visit bariatricfusion.com/product-safety for further details.

Products.Name: Bariatric Fusion High ADEK Multivitamin Capsules

Images.Caption: Recalled Bottle of Bariatric Fusion High ADEK Multivitamin
Recalled Bottle of Bariatric Fusion One Per Day Bariatric Multivitamin
Lot number 0066J4, 0065J4, 0453B5 or 0370B5 is printed on the bottom of the bottle

Manufacturers.Name: VitaQuest International, LLC of Caldwell, New Jersey

Retailers.Name: Available on Amazon.com, bariatricfusion.com, and other online platforms from January 2025 to June 2025, priced between $23 and $57.

Distributors.Name: Blueroot Health, Inc., of Middletown, Connecticut

Hazards.Name: Risk of child poisoning due to non-compliant packaging lacking child-resistant features.

Remedies.Name: Secure the recalled bottles away from children and contact Blueroot Health for a free child-resistant cap.

recalls.metaDescription: Blueroot Health recalls Bariatric Fusion Iron Multivitamins due to child poisoning risks. Learn how to secure your product safely.

recalls.Description: Creekwood Naturals is recalling its Gum Spirits of Turpentine bottles due to a serious risk of poisoning in children. These clear glass bottles feature a brown label with prominent red, black, and white text, clearly displaying the word "Turpentine" alongside "Pure Gum Spirits" or "Quality Gum Spirits." The packaging does not meet the required child-resistant standards, posing a significant danger to young children if ingested.
recalls.Title: Creekwood Naturals Recalls Turpentine Bottles Due to Child Poisoning Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Creekwood Naturals at 877-754-1776, Monday through Friday from 10 a.m. to 5 p.m. PT. Alternatively, they can email Sales@CreekwoodNaturals.com or visit https://CreekwoodNaturals.com/pages/recall for more details.

Images.Caption: Recalled Creekwood Naturals 100% Natural Pure Gum Spirits Turpentine bottle

Retailers.Name: Available on Amazon, Etsy, eBay, and Creekwood Naturals from June 2023 to May 2025, priced between $20 and $40.

Hazards.Name: Non-compliant packaging poses a poisoning risk to children.

Remedies.Name: Request a free replacement child-resistant closure.

recalls.metaDescription: Creekwood Naturals recalls turpentine bottles due to child poisoning risks. Contact for a free replacement closure.

recalls.Description:
Diamond G Forest Products is recalling its 100% pure gum spirits of turpentine, sold in 4-ounce clear glass bottles. These round bottles are labeled with "Georgia Pine Gum Spirits of Turpentine" and the company name. The packaging does not comply with child-resistant standards, creating a serious risk of poisoning if ingested by children.

Consumers are urged to keep these turpentine bottles out of children's reach and contact Diamond G Forest Products for details on obtaining a free child-resistant closure. This recall is essential to prevent potential injuries or fatalities related to accidental poisoning.
recalls.Title: Diamond G Forest Products Recalls Turpentine Bottles Due to Child Poisoning Hazard
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Diamond G Forest Products at 912-647-7463 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET Monday through Friday, email diamondgforestproducts@hotmail.com, or visit www.diamondgforestproducts.com/recall-information.

Products.Name: Georgia Pine Gum Spirits of Turpentine

Images.Caption: Recalled Diamond G Forest Products Georgia Pine Gum Spirits of Turpentine Bottle

Manufacturers.Name: Diamond G Forest Products LLC, of Patterson, Georgia

Retailers.Name: Nature's Corner Market in Marietta, GA; Cornerstone Retreat in Gap, PA; Nickel Mine Health Foods in Paradise, PA; Leaves of The Trees in Memphis, TN; Country Boy's Mini Mall in Bristol, GA; and online at Amazon.

Hazards.Name: Non-child-resistant packaging poses a poisoning risk to children.

Remedies.Name: Obtain a free replacement child-resistant closure.

recalls.metaDescription: Recall alert: Diamond G Forest Products' turpentine bottles pose a child poisoning risk. Learn how to secure a replacement closure.

recalls.Description: Walmart is recalling the Ozark Trail 64 oz Stainless Steel Insulated Water Bottles, identified by model number 83-662. These silver bottles feature a black one-piece screwcap lid and measure 4.41 x 4.41 x 11.5 inches, with an embedded Ozark Trail logo. The model number is not displayed on the product itself. The recall is due to a safety concern where the lid can eject forcefully, leading to potential impact and laceration injuries when attempting to open the bottle after storing certain beverages.
recalls.Title: Walmart Issues Recall for Ozark Trail 64 oz Water Bottles Due to Impact and Laceration Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For more information, consumers can reach Walmart at 800-925-6278 from 7 a.m. to 9 p.m. CT daily or visit www.walmart.com/help or https://corporate.walmart.com/recalls.

Products.Name: Ozark Trail 64 oz Stainless Steel Insulated Water Bottle

Images.Caption: Recalled Ozark Trail 64 oz Stainless-Steel Insulated Water Bottle, model 83-662
One-Piece Screwcap Lid

Injuries.Name: Walmart has received three reports of consumers who were injured when struck in the face by a lid forcefully ejected from these bottles upon opening. Two consumers suffered permanent vision loss after being struck in the eye.

Retailers.Name: Available at Walmart stores nationwide and online at Walmart.com since 2017 for approximately $15.

Importers.Name: Olympia Tools International Inc., of Covina, California

Hazards.Name: Risk of serious impact and laceration from the lid ejecting

Remedies.Name: Stop using the bottles and contact Walmart for a full refund; return to any Walmart store.

recalls.metaDescription: Walmart recalls Ozark Trail 64 oz Water Bottles due to serious safety risks. Learn how to get a refund and ensure your safety.

recalls.Description:
iHerb has announced a recall of three varieties of California Gold Nutrition dietary supplements: Daily Prenatal Multi, Ultamins Women's Multivitamin, and Ultamins Women's 50+ Multivitamin. The Daily Prenatal Multi comes in a white bottle with a gold border label, containing 60 fish gelatin softgels. The Ultamins Women's Multivitamin and Women's 50+ Multivitamin are packaged in dark purple containers with gold borders, each holding 60 capsules in foil packaging. Specific batch numbers and expiration dates are located on the back of each product's packaging.

The recalled supplements have been found to lack child-resistant packaging, violating the Poison Prevention Packaging Act. This poses a serious risk of poisoning if young children access the iron-containing contents. Consumers are urged to take immediate action to secure these products away from children.
recalls.Title: iHerb Issues Recall for California Gold Nutrition Iron Supplements Due to Child Poisoning Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach iHerb at 888-430-4770, email ProductRecall@iherb.com, or visit https://www.iherb.com/help/contact or https://information.iherb.com/hc/en-us/articles/37894456653972-iHerb-Recalls-California-Gold-Nutrition-Iron-Supplements-Due-to-Risk-of-Child-Poisoning.

Products.Name: California Gold Nutrition Daily Prenatal Multi

Images.Caption: Recalled Daily Prenatal Multi bottle
Label for Recalled Daily Prenatal Multi
Recalled Ultamins Women's Multivitamin package
Label for recalled Ultamins Women's Multivitamin package
Recalled Ultamins Women's 50+ Multivitamin package
Label for recalled Ultamins Women's 50+ Multivitamin package

Manufacturers.Name: iHerb LLC, dba Madre Labs LLC, of Irvine, California

Retailers.Name: Available online at Amazon.com, Walmart.com, Target.com, and other retailers from January 2019 to April 2025, priced between $8 and $18.

Hazards.Name: Risk of child poisoning due to non-child-resistant packaging of iron-containing dietary supplements.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should secure the recalled products out of children's reach and contact iHerb for a refund and disposal instructions.

recalls.metaDescription: iHerb recalls California Gold Nutrition Iron Supplements due to child poisoning risks. Secure products and contact for refunds.

recalls.Description: Kramer Laboratories is recalling Safetussin Max Strength Multi-Symptom Cough, Cold and Flu 24-count caplet blister packs due to a violation of federal regulations regarding child-resistant packaging. These products are identifiable by their labeling, which includes 'Safetussin,' 'Multi-Symptom,' and 'Cough, Cold & Flu,' and are packaged in a blue, orange, and red cardboard box with a drug facts label on the back. The packaging does not meet safety standards, posing a potential poisoning risk to young children if the tablets are accessed.
recalls.Title: Kramer Laboratories Issues Recall for Safetussin Max Strength Cough, Cold and Flu Packs Due to Child Safety Concerns
recalls.ConsumerContact: For further information, consumers can reach out to Kramer Laboratories at 800-824-4894 from Monday to Friday, 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, email kramerlabs@emersongroup.com, or visit https://safetussin.com/recall.

Products.Name: Safetussin Max Strength Cough, Cold and Flu Blister Packs

Images.Caption: Recalled Safetussin Max Strength Multi-Symptom Cough, Cold and Flu, 24-count caplets

Retailers.Name: Available at HEB, Harris-Teeter, and various regional grocery stores and independent pharmacies across the nation from July 2024 to March 2025 for approximately $11.

Distributors.Name: Kramer Laboratories, of Bridgewater, New Jersey (a subsidiary of Arcadia Consumer Healthcare, of Bridgewater, New Jersey)

Hazards.Name: Non-compliant packaging that poses a poisoning risk to children

Remedies.Name: Secure the product away from children and contact Kramer Laboratories for return or disposal instructions to obtain a full refund.

recalls.metaDescription: Kramer Laboratories recalls Safetussin Max Strength Cough, Cold and Flu packs due to child safety risks. Learn how to return for a refund.

recalls.Description:
Nutritional Fundamentals for Health (NFH) has issued a recall for its Iron SAP, Heme Iron SAP, and Prenatal SAP dietary supplement bottles due to non-compliance with federal child-resistant packaging regulations. The affected bottles are white, featuring the NFH brand name and a blue leaf logo, with date codes located on the bottom. These supplements are available in various sizes and have specific date codes that consumers should check against their products.

The recalled products include Iron SAP (Iron Glycinate), Heme Iron SAP (Porcine), and Prenatal SAP (Multivitamin) in different capsule counts. Consumers are urged to secure these products away from children to prevent accidental poisoning, as the packaging does not meet safety requirements mandated by the Poison Prevention Packaging Act (PPPA).
recalls.Title: Recall of NFH Iron Dietary Supplements Due to Child Safety Hazard
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach NFH toll-free at 866-510-3123 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, email info@nfh.ca, or visit https://nfh.ca/ and click on 'Recall' for further details.

Images.Caption: Recalled Iron SAP Iron Glycinate (120 capsules 1167U)
Recalled Iron SAP Iron Glycinate (60 Capsules 1029U)
Recalled Heme Iron SAP Superior Absorption and bioavailability (60 capsules 1124U)
Recalled Prenatal SAP Multivitamin (180 capsules 1034U)
Please see bottom of bottle for lot and expiration: (Example)

Manufacturers.Name: Nutritional Fundamentals for Health (dba NFH), of Canada

Retailers.Name: Available at various naturopathic and homeopathic clinics nationwide, A Woman's Time, Doctor Supplement stores, and online at WholescriptsInc.com and Fullscript.com from March 2022 through December 2024, priced between $20 and $95.

Importers.Name: Nutritional Fundamentals for Health (dba NFH), of Canada

Hazards.Name: Non-compliant packaging poses a poisoning risk to children.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should secure the products out of children's reach and contact NFH for a free replacement child-resistant bottle.

recalls.metaDescription: NFH recalls Iron SAP, Heme Iron SAP, and Prenatal SAP supplements due to child safety hazards. Secure products and contact NFH for replacements.

recalls.Description: Arsell is recalling its Benadryl Liquid Elixir, which comes in a 100 mL round dark plastic bottle featuring a pink and white label with 'Benadryl' printed in blue. The product is packaged in a decorative paper box and includes safety information on the back label. The packaging does not meet the child-resistant requirements mandated by the Poison Prevention Packaging Act, increasing the risk of accidental poisoning in young children if ingested.
recalls.Title: Arsell Issues Recall for Benadryl Liquid Elixir 100 mL Due to Child Poisoning Risk and Non-Compliant Packaging
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers should reach out to Arsell via email at recall@arsellsupport.com.

Images.Caption: Recalled Benadryl Liquid Elixir, 100 mL bottle and paper box with "X003VRIGUL" on the label

Retailers.Name: Available for purchase online at Amazon.com from July 2023 to October 2024, priced between $16 and $19.

Importers.Name: Arsell Inc., of Brooklyn, New York

Hazards.Name: Non-compliant child-resistant packaging poses a poisoning risk to children.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should keep the product out of children's reach and contact Arsell for a full refund. A photo showing the disposal of the recalled product and the Amazon order number will be required.

recalls.metaDescription: Arsell recalls Benadryl Liquid Elixir 100 mL due to child poisoning risk from non-compliant packaging. Contact for refunds.

recalls.Description: Gerolsteiner is recalling specific lots of their 750ml sparkling water bottles due to a potential laceration hazard. The affected bottles are sold individually or in cases of 15, featuring a white, blue, and red label with the brand name "Gerolsteiner." The lot numbers to look for are 11/28/2024 L and 11/27/2024 L, located on the lower part of the label.
recalls.Title: Gerolsteiner Sparkling Water Bottles Recalled Due to Laceration Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Gerolsteiner at 800-777-0633 from 8:30 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday. You can also email customerservice@consup.us or visit https://www.gerolsteiner.de/en/recall for more information.

Images.Caption: Recalled Gerolsteiner Sparkling Water (750ml)
Recalled Gerolsteiner Sparkling Water (750ml) lot number location

Manufacturers.Name: Gerolsteiner Brunnen GmbH & Co. KG, of Germany

Retailers.Name: Available at Trader Joe's locations across Alabama, Arkansas, Colorado, Florida, Georgia, Kansas, Louisiana, New Mexico, Oklahoma, South Carolina, Tennessee, and Texas from December 2024 through January 2025, priced around $3 each.

Hazards.Name: Laceration risk due to potential bottle cracking

Remedies.Name: Stop using the affected bottles and return them to the place of purchase for a full refund, no proof of purchase required.

recalls.metaDescription: Gerolsteiner recalls 750ml sparkling water bottles due to laceration hazards. Check lot numbers and contact for refunds.

recalls.Description: Taylor Water Technologies is recalling Phosphate Reagent #1 bottles sold with their Phosphate Test Kits (part number K-1106) and also available separately. These kits are designed for testing phosphate levels in water for pools, spas, and aquariums. The specific bottle containing sulfuric acid is marked with the product name 'Phosphate Reagent #1' and part number 'R-0980.' Each bottle has a shelf-life of 24 months.
recalls.Title: Taylor Water Technologies Recalls Phosphate Reagent #1 Bottles Due to Chemical Burn Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Taylor Water Technologies at 800-837-8548 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday to Friday, or reach out through their online contact form at https://www.taylortechnologies.com/en/content/contact-customer-service. More information can be found at https://www.taylortechnologies.com under 'Recall Information' in the Resource Center.

Images.Caption: Recalled Phosphate Reagent #1 bottle sold within the Taylor Phosphate Test Kits
Taylor Phosphate Test Kit with recalled Phosphate Reagent #1 bottle

Manufacturers.Name: Taylor Water Technologies LLC, of Sparks, Maryland

Retailers.Name: Available online at www.taylortechnologies.com and various pool and aquarium retailers across the country from August 2022 to August 2024, priced around $13 for the reagent bottles and $56 for the complete kits.

Hazards.Name: Risk of chemical burns due to non-child-resistant packaging of sulfuric acid

Remedies.Name: Stop using the recalled bottles, secure them away from children, and contact Taylor for disposal and replacement instructions.

recalls.metaDescription: Taylor Water Technologies recalls Phosphate Reagent #1 bottles due to chemical burn hazards. Learn how to get a free replacement.

recalls.Description:
Cabinet Health has issued a recall for specific 4 oz. Refillable Medicine Bottles featuring ABS plastic lids. These bottles, made of clear glass and available with blue, turquoise, orange, or purple lids labeled 'CABINET,' may not adequately protect against child access. The recalled products were sold filled with over-the-counter medications or as empty bottles. The empty bottles were available on cabinethealth.com from March 2023 to July 2024, while the filled bottles have specific date codes located on a sticker at the bottom of the bottle to help identify them.

Consumers who possess the recalled bottles should check the purchase date and the date codes of the filled bottles. If you have any of the affected products, it is crucial to secure them out of children's reach and contact Cabinet Health for a free replacement lid. This recall does not impact the medications contained within the bottles.
recalls.Title: Cabinet Health Recalls 4 oz. Refillable Medicine Bottles Due to Insufficient Child-Resistance, Risk of Poisoning
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Cabinet Health at 908-242-6108 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET Monday through Friday, email at recall@wearecabinet.com, or visit https://cabinethealth.com/voluntaryrecall or https://cabinethealth.com and click 'Recall' at the bottom of the page for further details.

Products.Name: Cabinet Health 4 oz. Refillable Medicine Bottles

Images.Caption: Recalled Cabinet Health 4 oz. Refillable Medicine Bottle
Recalled Cabinet Health 4 oz. Refillable Medicine Bottle
Recalled Cabinet Health 4 oz. Refillable Medicine Bottle
Recalled Cabinet Health 4 oz. Refillable Medicine Bottle
Recalled Cabinet Health 4 oz. Refillable Medicine Bottle
Recalled Cabinet Health 4 oz. Refillable Medicine Bottle

Manufacturers.Name: Cabinet Health Inc., of Washington, D.C.

Retailers.Name: Available online at cabinethealth.com, Amazon.com, and Grove.co, as well as at CVS stores from March 2023 through July 2024, priced between $10 and $20.

Hazards.Name: Inadequate child-resistance due to lid degradation, increasing the risk of poisoning if accessed by children.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should secure the recalled bottles away from children and contact Cabinet Health for a free replacement lid, providing a photo of the destroyed lid as proof.

recalls.metaDescription: Cabinet Health recalls 4 oz. refillable medicine bottles due to inadequate child-resistance, posing a poisoning risk. Secure and contact for replacement.

recalls.Description: The recall pertains to Euqee Wintergreen Essential Oil sold in 118 ml amber glass bottles with black screw-on caps. The front label features the text "Euqee, Essential Oil, Premium Grade, 100% Pure Plant Extract and Wintergreen," while the back includes safety information and storage guidelines. This product does not comply with the Poison Prevention Packaging Act due to its lack of child-resistant packaging, increasing the risk of poisoning for young children if ingested.
recalls.Title: Euqee Wintergreen Essential Oil Recalled for Poisoning Risk Due to Non-Child-Resistant Packaging
recalls.ConsumerContact: For further assistance, consumers should reach out to DLWL-US via email at gzzlhus@163.com, or visit www.euqee.net/page9 or www.euqee.net and click on "Recall" at the top of the page.

Images.Caption: Recalled Euqee Wintergreen Essential Oil

Retailers.Name: Available exclusively on Amazon.com from December 2023 through August 2024 for approximately $8.

Distributors.Name: DingLinWangLuoKeJiGuang ZhouYouXianGongSi, dba DLWL-US, of China

Hazards.Name: Non-child-resistant packaging poses a poisoning risk to children due to the presence of methyl salicylate.

Remedies.Name: Consumers are advised to keep the recalled oil away from children and contact DLWL-US for a full refund. A pre-paid return label and packaging will be provided for the return of the product.

recalls.metaDescription: Euqee Wintergreen Essential Oil recalled for poisoning risk due to non-child-resistant packaging. Contact for refunds and returns.

recalls.Description:
BRS portable liquid fuel bottles are being recalled due to safety concerns. These bottles, available in 530mL, 750mL, and 1000mL sizes, are orange with a black cap and feature white lettering indicating the brand, volume, and safety warnings. The product does not comply with the child-resistant closure requirements mandated by the Children's Gasoline Burn Prevention Act, which raises serious burn and poisoning risks for children.

Consumers are urged to stop using these bottles immediately and keep them out of children's reach. For safe disposal instructions and to obtain a full refund, contact Huenco directly.
recalls.Title: Recall of BRS Liquid Fuel Bottles Due to Poisoning and Burn Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: Email at chencaiyu1994@hotmail.com, or visit their Amazon seller profile at Amazon.com Seller Profile: Huenco.

Images.Caption: Recalled BRS liquid fuel bottles - front
Recalled BRS liquid fuel bottles – back
Recalled BRS liquid fuel bottles – back

Retailers.Name: Available on Amazon.com from October 2018 through December 2023 for approximately $15.

Distributors.Name: Huenco, of China

Hazards.Name: Non-compliance with child-resistant closure requirements, posing burn and poisoning risks to children.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the bottles, keep them away from children, and contact Huenco for disposal instructions and a full refund.

recalls.metaDescription: Recall of BRS Liquid Fuel Bottles due to burn and poisoning hazards. Immediate action required for safety.

recalls.Description: Yoto Ltd. is recalling the Yoto Mini, a portable, screen-free speaker designed for children aged three to twelve. This device, which operates using Yoto cards, plays audiobooks, music, radio, and podcasts. The speakers feature a pale gray color with orange tactile controls and a pixel display. The model name Yoto Mini, along with SKU PRPLXX00860, can be found on the base of the unit. The recalled speakers were sold with a charging cable but did not include an adaptor or charger.
recalls.Title: Yoto Mini Speakers for Kids Recalled Due to Fire and Burn Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Yoto Ltd. at 844-370-0426 during business hours from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday, via email at minihelp@yotoplay.com, or visit https://yotoplay.com/recall. You can also go to https://us.yotoplay.com/ and select "RECALL INFORMATION" at the top of the page for further details.

Products.Name: Yoto Mini Speakers
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 251,165 (In addition, about 18,932 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Yoto Mini
Base of Recalled Yoto Mini

Injuries.Name: Yoto has received six reports from U.S. consumers and one from a UK consumer of the speaker overheating or melting. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available online at us.yotoplay.com, Amazon.com, Target.com, and maisonette.com, as well as in toy and gift stores across the country from November 2021 to April 2024 for approximately $70.

Hazards.Name: Lithium-ion battery overheating, leading to burn and fire hazards

Remedies.Name: Stop using the speakers immediately, keep them away from children, and contact Yoto for a free replacement charging cable. Send a photo of the old cable cut in half to receive the new cord.

recalls.metaDescription: Yoto recalls Yoto Mini speakers for kids due to fire hazards. Learn how to get a free replacement charging cable.

recalls.Description: Procter & Gamble has issued a recall for specific lot codes of Tide Pods, Gain Flings, Ace Pods, and Ariel Pods liquid laundry detergent packets. These products, packaged in flexible film bags, were produced between September 2023 and February 2024. The recalled packets contain between 12 to 39 detergent units and include various scents and formulations, such as Gain Flings Original and Tide Pods Free & Gentle.
recalls.Title: Procter & Gamble Recalls 8.2 Million Tide, Gain, Ace, and Ariel Laundry Detergent Packets Due to Safety Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Procter & Gamble at 833-347-5764 from Monday to Friday, 9 AM ET to 6 PM ET, and Saturday, 9 AM ET to 5:30 PM ET. More information is available online at pg.com/bags.

Products.Name: Tide Pods
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 8.2 million (In addition, about 56,741 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Tide Pods
Recalled Gain laundry packets
Recalled Ace Pods
Recalled Ariel laundry packets

Injuries.Name: No confirmed cases directly relating to this packaging defect. The firm has received four reports of children in the United States accessing the liquid laundry packets, three of which reported ingestion during the time period that the recalled lots were sold, but it is not known if these laundry packets came from recalled bags.

Manufacturers.Name: The Procter & Gamble Company, of Cincinnati, Ohio

Retailers.Name: Big Lots

Hazards.Name: The packaging may open near the zipper track, posing a serious risk of ingestion and potential injuries to children and vulnerable individuals. Ingesting large amounts of these products can be fatal, especially for those with pre-existing health conditions.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should secure the recalled bags away from children and contact Procter & Gamble for a full refund and a complimentary child-resistant storage bag. A cabinet lock for securing laundry products is also available. To check if their product is affected, consumers can verify the lot code on the bottom of the package at pg.com/bags. Photos of the recalled product with the lot code can be submitted to participate in the recall.

recalls.metaDescription: Procter & Gamble recalls 8.2 million Tide, Gain, Ace, and Ariel laundry detergent packets due to safety hazards. Check if your product is affected.

recalls.Description: The recall affects BRS and BULin portable liquid fuel bottles, which were sold individually. The BRS model is orange with a black cap, while the BULin model is red with a matching red cap. Both bottles are available in a 1000mL size and feature text in Chinese, including the brand names and safety warnings in white lettering.
recalls.Title: Recall of BRS and BULin Liquid Fuel Bottles Due to Burn and Poisoning Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, please email squarebird@aliyun.com or visit their Amazon seller profile at https://www.amazon.com/s?me=A1778LF2VCKZ0J&marketplaceID=ATVPDKIKX0DER.

Recalled BRS liquid fuel bottle – back

Recalled BULin liquid fuel bottle – back

Retailers.Name: Available exclusively on Amazon.com from January 2023 to December 2023, priced between $10 and $13.

Distributors.Name: Tentock, of China

Hazards.Name: Non-child-resistant closures leading to burn and poisoning risks for children.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the recalled bottles immediately, keep them out of children's reach, and contact Tentock for disposal instructions to obtain a full refund.

recalls.metaDescription: BRS and BULin liquid fuel bottles recalled due to burn and poisoning hazards. Immediate action required for safety.
